heading: Establishment and expansion of Regional Technology Officer Program
label: 10305 Establishment and expansion of Regional Technology Officer Program
notes: Amendments2024—Subsec. (a)(1). inserted “, and shall be administered by the Bureau for Cyberspace and Digital Policy” before period at end.Pub. L. 118–159

DefinitionsFor definitions of “Secretary”, “appropriate congressional committees”, and “Department” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 9002 of Pub. L. 117–263section 2651 of this title
amendments: Amendments2024—Subsec. (a)(1). inserted “, and shall be administered by the Bureau for Cyberspace and Digital Policy” before period at end.Pub. L. 118–159

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program, which shall be known as the “Regional Technology Officer Program” (referred to in this section as the “Program”), and shall be administered by the Bureau for Cyberspace and Digital Policy.

children.label: A
children.text: Promoting United States leadership in technology abroad.

children.label: B
children.text: Working with partners to increase the deployment of critical and emerging technology in support of democratic values.

children.label: C
children.text: Shaping diplomatic agreements in regional and international fora with respect to critical and emerging technologies.

children.label: D
children.text: Building diplomatic capacity for handling critical and emerging technology issues.

children.label: E
children.text: Facilitating the role of critical and emerging technology in advancing the foreign policy objectives of the United States through engagement with research labs, incubators, and venture capitalists.

children.label: F
children.text: Maintaining the advantages of the United States with respect to critical and emerging technologies.
advancing the goals described in subsection (a)(2);
hiring Regional Technology Officers and increasing the competitiveness of the Program within the Foreign Service bidding process;
expanding the Program to include a minimum of 15 Regional Technology Officers; and

children.label: A
children.text: each regional bureau of the Department; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Bureau of International Organization Affairs.

children.heading: Annual briefing requirement
children.label: c Annual briefing requirement
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and annually thereafter for the following 5 years, the Secretary shall brief the appropriate congressional committees regarding the status of the implementation plan required under subsection (b).December 23, 2022

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated up to $25,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2023 through 2027 to carry out this section.
